content:
Member-only story

The Select Projection Pattern That Eliminates 90% of Your .Include() Chains

8 min read
Just now

--

Press enter or click to view image in full size

You’ve seen this query. You’ve written this query. It’s the “get everything just in case” approach that starts innocent and becomes a performance disaster:
var orders = await context.Orders
.Include(o => o.Customer)
.ThenInclude(c => c.Address)
.Include(o => o.OrderItems)
.ThenInclude(oi => oi.Product)
.ThenInclude(p => p.Category)
.Include(o => o.ShippingDetails)
.ThenInclude(s => s.Carrier)
.Where(o => o.Status == OrderStatus.Pending)
.ToListAsync();
// Then you map manually in memory
var dtos = orders.Select(o => new OrderDto
{
Id = o.Id,
CustomerName = o.Customer.Name,
CustomerEmail = o.Customer.Email,
TotalAmount = o.OrderItems.Sum(oi => oi.Quantity * oi.UnitPrice),
ItemCount = o.OrderItems.Count,
ShippingAddress = o.ShippingDetails?.Address,
CarrierName = o.ShippingDetails?.Carrier?.Name
}).ToList();This query loads entire entity graphs into memory — Customer.Address, every OrderItem, every Product, every Category — just to discard 90% of the data in a LINQ-to-Objects projection. The database transfers megabytes. EF Core tracks entities you don't need. Your memory pressure spikes.
